delegationRole: The U.S. delegation, insisting on irreversible denuclearization inside a comprehensive package before any relief, while offering an uncoupled bilateral channel to Pyongyang as confidence-building.
fundamentalPrinciples: Establishment of peace, stability and security on the Korean Peninsula.
North Korea must become a responsible, non-threatening, independent nation — or work in good faith with South Korea to reunify the peninsula.
Bad behavior will not be rewarded.

desiredEndStates.primary: Irreversible denuclearization of the DPRK and termination of its long-range missile program, verified internationally, inside a comprehensive agreement that also ends all proliferation and returns the North to the NPT and the IAEA.
desiredEndStates.alternate: If the comprehensive package stalls, sustained trilateral pressure plus an uncoupled U.S.–DPRK dialogue without preconditions, keeping sanctions, THAAD and exercises fully in place.

keyPositionsByIssue.DISARMAMENT: Internationally monitored shutdown and irreversible disablement of the nuclear program, verified termination of medium- and long-range ballistic missiles, and reduction of the military-industrial complex to normal self-defense levels.
keyPositionsByIssue.MUTUAL-FREEZE: Reject outright. A freeze is potentially unverifiable and would lock in the capabilities Pyongyang already has; cancelling ROK-U.S. training for an undetermined period would degrade readiness. It is not the neutral event Beijing claims.
keyPositionsByIssue.THAAD: The current level of JP-ROK-U.S. exercises and THAAD will be retained and sustained as long as the North's nuclear and missile programs exist.
keyPositionsByIssue.SANCTIONS-HUMANITARIAN: No sanctions relief and no assistance beyond the most urgent humanitarian need until Pyongyang has agreed to, and verifiably implemented, a comprehensive package covering every issue in the Chairman's Framework.
keyPositionsByIssue.DIPLOMATIC-RELATIONS: If the atmosphere improves considerably, Pyongyang, Seoul and Washington will open separate negotiations on a peace treaty; a peace declaration is possible if the North does not use it to erode the U.S.–ROK alliance.
keyPositionsByIssue.PROLIFERATION: A monitored and verified end to all DPRK proliferation — nuclear, ballistic, chemical and cyber — must be part of the comprehensive agreement, with the regime held accountable for chemical transfers and cyber attacks.
keyPositionsByIssue.LWR-KEDO: Little interest, but we will not oppose the discussion. If pursued, only through a re-established KEDO with the DPRK given no access to the reactors; we prefer that China and Russia supply conventional power and rehabilitate the grid.
keyPositionsByIssue.PEACEFUL-NUCLEAR-ENERGY: Any civilian program presupposes return to the NPT as a non-nuclear weapons state in good standing, readmission to the IAEA, and unconditional acceptance of all Agency monitoring.
keyPositionsByIssue.INTER-KOREAN: Support resumed inter-Korean dialogue without preconditions, kept as separate from the Six-Party process as possible.
keyPositionsByIssue.ABDUCTIONS: We sympathize with Tokyo and will lend support to its arguments, while pushing Japan and the DPRK toward separate bilateral talks.

redLines: Any bad behavior or military brinkmanship on the part of the North Korean government.
Movement away from a comprehensive agreement without the establishment of a satisfactory alternative plan.
Any indication that North Korea would be allowed to maintain any part of its nuclear program or ballistic missile systems.
batna: Maintain the pressure architecture indefinitely — full sanctions, THAAD, trilateral exercises and extended deterrence — and let the talks lapse rather than reward an unverifiable freeze.
concessionsWilling: Open U.S.–DPRK dialogue without preconditions, uncoupled from the Six-Party Talks, as confidence-building.
Support a peace declaration and eventual peace treaty once the atmosphere and verification permit.
Decline to oppose light water reactor discussions through a re-established KEDO with no DPRK access to the reactors.
Invite China and Russia to supply the DPRK with conventional electrical power and grid rehabilitation.
Provide the most urgent humanitarian assistance, and full assistance once a comprehensive agreement is implemented.
Back a Japan–DPRK bilateral track on the abduction issue.
negotiatingStyle: Verification-first and conditional: refuses to pay for process, converts every concession into a sequencing question, and keeps the trilateral bloc visibly locked.
privateInstructions: Work in unison with Seoul and Tokyo to hold the joint position on a comprehensive agreement covering every issue in the Chairman's Framework.
If the comprehensive approach shows no promise, do not let that compromise the joint position on abolishing the nuclear and missile programs.
Propose resumed U.S.–DPRK dialogue without preconditions, uncoupled from these talks, to rebuild trust.
Refuse the 'mutual freeze' explicitly: unverifiable, readiness-degrading, and a net gain for Pyongyang.
Insist that non-proliferation, and the North's chemical and cyber capabilities, be addressed even though they sit outside the Chairman's Framework.
If LWRs are pursued, insist on KEDO with no DPRK access; otherwise invite Beijing and Moscow to build conventional power.
Watch for the North using any bilateral channel as a pretense to erode the U.S.–ROK alliance.
Route periodic reports and requests for guidance to the office of the Secretary of State.
